body.title: Design doc: Pursuit as Igor's goal-bound behavioral layer above engrams
body.result: Three-layer design doc (Cells → Engrams → Pursuits) with biology refs (Schultz, Graybiel, TOTE, Dickinson-Balleine). Primitives, explains BOREDOM+reply bugs, open questions for test-case ticket. lab/design_docs/pursuit_layer.md + lab/design_docs_for_igor/pursuit_layer.dsb (machine-readable). Commit a1fcbcc4.
body.description:
Capture the full architectural design of the Pursuit layer for Igor's cognitive hierarchy.

Doc must cover:

1. Three-layer model
   - Cells: engram primitives (BRANCHIF, FORKIF, SPAWNIF, EMITIF — 'assembly')
   - Engrams: behavior chains ('high-level code')
   - Pursuits: goal-bound orchestration of engrams ('behavioral processes')
   Pursuits USE engrams; they are not a template OF engrams. Different organizational level.

2. What a Pursuit IS
   - Entry stimulus (what triggers commitment)
   - Goal-facia formation at commitment (the 'I am now pursuing X' moment)
   - Anticipatory dopamine trace (not just at completion — at commitment)
   - Engram-substrate action(s) in service of the goal
   - Recursive sub-Pursuit spawning (Pursuit invokes Pursuit)
   - Completion test (did the goal resolve?)
   - Dopamine ding on resolution

3. Goals are everywhere
   - Not just big ambitions — every act of commitment is a Pursuit
   - 'Reply to Akien' is a Pursuit. 'Scan for work' is a Pursuit. 'Claim T-x' is a Pursuit.
   - Nested: top-level Pursuits spawn sub-Pursuits, each with its own dopamine events

4. Biology references
   - Schultz/Dayan/Daw: dopamine = prediction error, fires at anticipation not just reward
   - Miller-Galanter-Pribram (1960): TOTE units — recursive Test-Operate-Test-Exit
   - Sutton-Precup: Options (CS analog) — temporally-extended action with initiation/policy/termination
   - Graybiel: action chunks in striatum (habitual side)
   - Dickinson-Balleine: goal-directed vs habitual action — Pursuits are goal-directed layer
   - Cortico-basal-ganglia loops as architectural analog: BG recruits cortex, Pursuits recruit engrams

5. Relationship to bugs being fixed
   - BOREDOM chain: was stimulus→action (no commitment trace) → foreman pickup fails
   - Reply-eats-progress: reply fires without Pursuit → no continuation goal → attention drops
   - Both fail same way: action without goal-adoption

File location: lab/design_docs/ (or lab/docs/ per DSB convention — check before writing).
Gates: T-pursuit-engram-programming-docs (the programming docs build on this).

Test plan:
- All 5 sections present and complete
- Biology citations verifiable
- Bug explanation section clear to future CC sessions post-compact
- Doc reviewed by Akien before closing
